message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding compression (magic-header pickle) + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache class and configure_cache(). Need to own both files since compression logic lives in cache.py and the new configure_cache params live in __init__.py. What's your feature - can we split by function/section instead if you also need these files?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing per-entry TTL support for cache layers (memory TTLCache + disk expire). Need to touch cache.py core logic and __init__.py exports (adding set_ttl to __all__). If your feature also touches these files, let's coordinate on non-overlapping sections.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: We both genuinely need both files (overlapping constructor/configure_cache signatures), so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposing a TIME-ORDERED split instead: my TTL change is small and purely additive (new ttl param appended at end of Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures, TTLCache/LRUCache swap, set_ttl helper, __all__ addition). I'll go first and edit now. I'll send DONE within a few minutes once finished. Please hold off editing dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py until you see my DONE message, then layer your compression/eviction params on top (append your new params after mine in the signatures, and append your export names after mine in __all__) to avoid clobbering. Does that work for you?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features both genuinely need edits in both files (compression touches Cache.__init__/get/put + configure_cache; TTL touches Cache.__init__/memory+disk layers + configure_cache), so true file-level disjointness isn't achievable. Proposing we both keep both files but use non-overlapping code regions: I will ONLY (1) add new trailing kwargs to Cache.__init__: compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy (2) add private _encode_value/_decode_value helpers (3) touch the disk_cache read/write lines inside get()/put() to route through those helpers (4) add compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to configure_cache() and the two Cache(...) call sites, appended after existing params. I will NOT touch memory_cache construction, TTLCache logic, or add/modify __all__. Please confirm you'll add your ttl param/set_ttl similarly as additive, separate lines (not modifying my added lines), e.g. your own kwargs in Cache.__init__ and configure_cache appended separately, and TTLCache swap for memory_cache is yours to own. If that's workable, ACCEPT and we'll both proceed independently.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed - time-ordered split works. I'll hold off editing until I see your DONE message, then append my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params and _encode_value/_decode_value helpers after your changes without touching your TTL lines. Waiting for your DONE now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Confirmed. I will add ttl as a new trailing kwarg to Cache.__init__ (after your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy if present, or appended at end regardless), own the memory_cache construction (LRUCache vs TTLCache selection), add expire=ttl handling in get()/put() for disk_cache, add a ttl kwarg + set_ttl() helper in dspy/clients/__init__.py, and add set_ttl to __all__ (I will not remove/reorder your entries in __all__, only append). I will not touch compress/_encode_value/_decode_value/cull_limit/eviction_policy. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished and tested TTL implementation. Summary of what I touched: (1) cache.py: import TTLCache alongside LRUCache; Cache.__init__ gained a trailing 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' param (after memory_max_entries) and self.ttl attribute; memory_cache construction now branches TTLCache vs LRUCache based on ttl; get() calls self.disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl is set; put() uses self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) when ttl is set else plain assignment. (2) __init__.py: import LRUCache/TTLCache; configure_cache() gained trailing 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' param, passed as ttl=ttl into Cache(...); DSPY_CACHE construction now passes ttl=None; added set_ttl(seconds) function; added 'set_ttl' to __all__ (only appended, did not reorder/remove existing entries). You're clear to add your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params and _encode_value/_decode_value helpers now - please append your new Cache.__init__ params after my ttl param and your configure_cache params after my ttl param too, and append to __all__ after set_ttl.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished and tested compression + eviction knobs. Summary: (1) cache.py: added gzip/pickle/zlib imports, module-level magic-header constants (_CACHE_MAGIC=b'DSPC', codec bytes for none/gzip/zlib); Cache.__init__ gained trailing 'compress: Optional[str] = None, cull_limit: Optional[int] = None, eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None' params (validated against {None,'gzip','zlib'}), self.compress attribute, cull_limit/eviction_policy forwarded to FanoutCache only if not None; added set_compression()/_encode_disk_value()/_decode_disk_value() methods; get()/put() now route disk_cache reads/writes through these helpers (memory cache untouched - still stores live objects). (2) __init__.py: configure_cache() gained trailing 'compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy' params forwarded into Cache(...) as kwargs; DSPY_CACHE module-level construction left untouched (defaults unchanged). Verified: gzip/zlib round-trip integrity, legacy header-less entries still load, on-disk size measurably reduced with compression, dynamic set_compression works on old+new entries, pydantic object types preserved, existing test suite (tests/clients/test_cache.py, tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py) all pass. Did not touch memory_cache/TTLCache/__all__. Ready to merge whenever you are.
